title: Physics Matching Drill
description: Practice drag-and-drop and multiple-choice questions with core physics concepts.

questions.question: Match each mechanics quantity to its standard formula.
questions.hint: Look for the formula most commonly linked to each quantity.
questions.explanation: These are standard introductory mechanics relationships.

questions.question: Match each electromagnetic wave to a common application.
questions.hint: Think about long-distance signals, heat detection, and high-energy imaging.
questions.explanation: Each wave band is commonly associated with one of these technologies.

questions.question: Match each optical element to its usual effect on parallel rays.
questions.hint: One element brings rays together, one spreads them apart, and one only reflects them.
questions.explanation: Convex lenses converge, concave lenses diverge, and plane mirrors reflect without changing convergence.

questions.question: Which SI unit measures electric current?
questions.hint: It is named after Andre-Marie Ampere.
questions.explanation: Electric current is measured in amperes.
